title: Spiced Crab Croquetas
description: Crispy fried Spanish croquettes featuring a blend of white and brown crab meat spiced with fennel seeds and pimentón.
instructions: Melt butter in a non-stick pan over medium heat, stir in flour, and cook for one to two minutes.
Add pimentón and crushed fennel seeds, then gradually pour in 550ml of milk while stirring continuously.
Cook for seven minutes until a thick, glossy sauce forms, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for two to three additional minutes, adding extra milk if the mixture becomes too thick.
Remove sauce from heat, combine with the crab meat and lemon zest, then spread evenly onto a lined baking tray.
Cover the surface directly with greaseproof paper, cool to room temperature, and refrigerate for a minimum of two hours.
Roll the chilled mixture with wet hands into approximately 30 small balls.
Dip each portion into the beaten egg, coat thoroughly with panko breadcrumbs, and place back onto the tray.
Freeze the coated shapes for 20 minutes to firm up.
Heat frying oil to 170°C and fry the portions in small batches for three to four minutes until golden and hot.
Drain and finish with a light dusting of sea salt before serving.

title: Perfect Risotto Primavera
description: A creamy Italian rice dish packed with blanched spring greens and cooked in a homemade vegetable stock infused with pod and shoot trimmings.
instructions: Shell the peas and broad beans, saving the empty pods.
Remove the tough ends from the asparagus spears and reserve them, then chop the remaining stems into small pieces while keeping the tips whole.
Clean the leek, slicing the main body into thin ribbons and setting aside the trimmings.
Boil the chopped asparagus pieces and tips in a pot of salted water for two minutes.
Add the peas and broad beans to the pot, cook for two more minutes until tender, then transfer all the vegetables into ice-cold water using a slotted spoon.
Place the saved vegetable trimmings, pods, asparagus ends, and one crushed garlic clove into the vegetable cooking water, then simmer gently for 30 minutes to create a stock.
Melt half the butter in a separate pan over low heat, add the sliced leek ribbons along with the remaining sliced garlic, and cook until softened without browning.
Increase the heat, stir in the rice to coat it thoroughly in the butter, and cook until the grains are hot to the touch.
Pour in the white wine and cook until the liquid is fully absorbed by the rice.
Add the warm homemade stock one ladleful at a time, stirring continuously and allowing the liquid to absorb before adding the next batch, until the rice is just cooked.
Incorporate the final ladle of stock, then stir in the remaining butter, grated cheese, and the drained green vegetables.
Cover the pan, remove it from the heat source, and let it rest for two minutes before mixing and garnishing with fresh mint.

title: Midnight Berry Smoothie
description: An antioxidant-rich boost featuring blueberries, blackberries, and a hint of mint.
instructions: Place berries, banana, and mint leaves into a blender.
Add almond milk, Greek yogurt, and honey.
Blend on high until completely smooth.
Pour into a chilled glass and garnish with an extra mint leaf.

title: Orange, grapefruit and bay jelly
description: A citrus-based jelly infused with fresh bay leaves and served alongside softly whipped double cream.
instructions: Lightly oil a 1 litre mould.
Strain juices into a saucepan, add bruised bay leaves and sugar, simmer until sugar dissolves, then remove from heat to infuse for at least 30 minutes.
Soften gelatine leaves in cold water for five minutes, then squeeze out excess water.
Discard bay leaves, warm the juice, stir in the gelatine until dissolved, pour into the mould and chill for four to six hours until set.
Briefly dip the mould in warm water, turn the jelly out onto a plate and serve with softly whipped cream.

title: Matcha bounty bars
description: A homemade confectionery featuring a matcha-infused desiccated coconut filling coated in melted dark chocolate.
ingredients: 3 tbsp maple syrup or runny honey
instructions: Line a 20cm square baking tin with baking paper.
Combine softened creamed coconut, coconut oil, maple syrup, vanilla, matcha and sea salt in a bowl, adding two to four tablespoons of water for a softer texture if desired.
Stir in the desiccated coconut, press the mixture evenly into the prepared tin and refrigerate for 15 to 30 minutes until set.
Melt the chocolate in a heatproof bowl set over a pan of simmering water.
Slice the coconut mixture into 24 bars, coat each bar in the melted chocolate using two forks, place on a lined tray and chill for 15 to 30 minutes before sprinkling with flaky salt.

title: Grilled Nectarine and Burrata Salad
description: A fresh summer salad featuring charred nectarines, creamy burrata cheese and quick-pickled red onions tossed with tomatoes, cucumber and rocket.
ingredients: 1 peeled and finely sliced red onion
1 cucumber, halved lengthways, deseeded and sliced diagonally
instructions: Mix the red onion, white wine vinegar and caster sugar in a bowl, season and leave to pickle.
Toast the pumpkin seeds in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for a few minutes then remove from the pan.
Stone the nectarines, cut into quarters, toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, season and char in a hot griddle pan for a few minutes on each side.
Toss the cucumber, tomatoes, mint, rocket, lemon juice and a few tablespoons of olive oil together in a bowl, season well and transfer to a serving plate.
Tear the burrata over the salad, arrange the nectarines, scatter the pickled onions along with their liquid, drizzle with remaining olive oil and top with pumpkin seeds.
